vocabulary.definition: a small space for storing things or sleeping
vocabulary.example: because the cupboard under the stairs was full of them, and that was where he slept.

vocabulary.definition: the son of your brother or sister
vocabulary.example: Nearly ten years had passed since the Dursleys had woken up to find their nephew on the front step, but Privet Drive had hardly changed at all.

vocabulary.definition: a sudden angry outburst, especially in a child
vocabulary.example: Harry, who could see a huge Dudley tantrum coming on, began wolfing down his bacon as fast as possible in case Dudley turned the table over.

vocabulary.definition: a mark left on the skin after a wound heals
vocabulary.example: The only thing Harry liked about his own appearance was a very thin scar on his forehead that was shaped like a bolt of lightning.
His aunt and uncle never spoke about them, and of course he was forbidden to ask questions.
He'd lived with the Dursleys almost ten years, ten miserable years, as long as he could remember, ever since he'd been a baby and his parents had died in that car crash.

vocabulary.definition: a cold-blooded animal such as a snake or lizard
vocabulary.example: After lunch they went to the reptile house.

vocabulary.definition: to close one eye briefly as a signal or joke

vocabulary.example: Harry sat up and gasped; the glass front of the boa constrictor's tank had vanished.

vocabulary.definition: to move smoothly like a snake
vocabulary.example: The great snake was uncoiling itself rapidly, slithering out onto the floor.
The zoo director himself made Aunt Petunia a cup of strong, sweet tea while he apologized over and over again.
Aunt Petunia had decided it must have shrunk in the wash and, to his great relief, Harry wasn't punished.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: A skinny ten-year-old who sleeps in a cupboard and is bullied by Dudley.

characters.name: Dudley Dursley
characters.description: Harry's spoiled cousin who expects dozens of birthday presents.

characters.name: Aunt Petunia & Uncle Vernon
characters.description: Harry's guardians who treat him badly and fear anything unusual.

characters.name: Mrs. Figg
characters.description: The neighbor who usually watches Harry; her broken leg sends him to the zoo.

background.hook: Ten years after arriving at Privet Drive, Harry still lives in a cupboard under the stairs. On Dudley's birthday trip to the zoo, he discovers he can talk to a snake — and the glass in front of the tank vanishes.
background.readingFocus: Notice how the Dursleys treat Harry compared to Dudley. Watch for small signs that strange things happen around Harry.

miniCheck.stem: To feel miserable means to feel —

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry's unhappy life with the Dursleys and strange events at the zoo on Dudley's birthday
A Quidditch match at the zoo

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers ten years at Privet Drive, Dudley's birthday outing, and the vanishing glass at the reptile house.

comprehension.stem: Where does Harry sleep at the Dursleys' house?

options.text: In a cupboard under the stairs

comprehension.explanation: Harry sleeps in the cupboard under the stairs, which is full of spiders.

comprehension.stem: Why does Harry get to go to the zoo with the Dursleys?

options.text: Mrs. Figg broke her leg and could not watch him
Uncle Vernon wanted to reward him

comprehension.explanation: Mrs. Figg usually looks after Harry on Dudley's birthday, but she broke her leg.

comprehension.stem: What happens to the glass in front of the boa constrictor's tank?
comprehension.explanation: The glass front of the tank had vanished and the snake slithered out.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry feel when he learns Mrs. Figg cannot take him?

options.text: Glad — his heart gives a leap

comprehension.explanation: Dudley's mouth fell open in horror, but Harry's heart gave a leap.

comprehension.stem: Why is Uncle Vernon furious with Harry at the end of the chapter?

options.text: Strange things happened at the zoo and Piers said Harry was talking to the snake
The motorcycle in Harry's dream was real

comprehension.explanation: After the glass vanished, Piers said Harry was talking to the snake; Vernon sends Harry to the cupboard with no meals.

comprehension.stem: What does the boa constrictor do that surprises Harry?
comprehension.explanation: The snake opened its beady eyes, raised its head, and winked at Harry.

vocabulary.explanation: Harry could see a huge Dudley tantrum coming on when Dudley counted his presents.

vocabulary.stem: Harry was used to spiders because the ___ under the stairs was full of them, and that was where he slept.
vocabulary.explanation: Harry's bedroom is the cupboard under the stairs.
The glass front of the boa constrictor's tank had vanished.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'miserable'?
vocabulary.explanation: Harry describes ten miserable years living with the Dursleys.

vocabulary.stem: After lunch they went to the ___ house, where Harry spoke to a boa constrictor.
vocabulary.explanation: The reptile house is where the snake tank is located.

cloze.title: The Vanishing Glass
paragraphs: Ten years after baby Harry arrived on Privet Drive, he still slept in a (1) ___ under the stairs. On Dudley's birthday, Mrs. Figg could not watch him, so Harry went to the zoo instead.
In the (2) ___ house, a boa constrictor (3) ___ at Harry. Suddenly the glass (4) ___ and the snake (5) ___ out onto the floor.
The zoo director (6) ___ to Aunt Petunia while Uncle Vernon sent Harry to the cupboard with no meals. Harry remembered ten (7) ___ years with the Dursleys.

gaps.explanation: Harry sleeps in the cupboard under the stairs.
After lunch they went to the reptile house.
The snake winked at Harry through the glass.
The glass front of the tank had vanished.
The snake slithered out onto the floor.
The zoo director apologized over and over again.
Harry had lived ten miserable years with the Dursleys.
